title: phaseolus vulgaris var. fulvoater
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. fulvoater, also known as the variegated kidney bean or red mottled kidney bean, is a variety of the common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) characterized by its distinctive red and white mottled pattern. The plant is primarily valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The beans from this plant are rich in nutrients and have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the beans can be dried and ground into a powder or used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness their medicinal properties.
